findings: Identity is correct: the main bird matches Song Sparrow proportions and plumage—rounded head, short stout gray bill, dark eye, grayish/russet-striped face, heavily streaked brown upperparts, bold breast streaking with a central concentration, long rounded tail, and pinkish-brown legs. Its two legs, one tail, one head, and one beak are anatomically coherent; the far wing is occluded by the near wing in this side view.

All printed claims match the independently checked Cornell measurements: length 12–17 cm (4.7–6.7 in), wingspan 18–24 cm (7.1–9.4 in), and weight 12–53 g (0.4–1.9 oz). Family, scientific name, habitat, foraging/diet, song description, and field marks are supported by Cornell and Audubon.

Minor presentation issue only: the detached head and wing studies are useful field-plate insets, but they can momentarily read as extra anatomy rather than labeled detail studies. This is not a species, text, or main-bird anatomy error.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears on the candidate.

verification_sources.title: Song Sparrow Identification | All About Birds
